steps.title: Check current akri deployment
steps.description:
Verify your akri version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n akri -l app.kubernetes.io/name=akri
helm list -n akri 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working akri installation.

steps.title: Review akri configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant akri configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n akri -l app.kubernetes.io/name=akri
kubectl get configmap -n akri -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=akri
```
A proposal.

Part "can I repro 'Nessie'?".
Part "What could be even simpler?".

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Extensibility] HTTP protocol
steps.description:
Sorry , we have been putting out some fires ... this is still a priority for us to get in!  We have a goal of having someone walk through all of the steps and get it running this week.  Other than any clarifications that come from that exercise, the remaining task will be to migrate the 2 md files (docs/extensibility-http.md & sample/apps/http-apps/README.md) in extensibility-http into the extensibility.md file in main (like extensibility.md does now: describing the changes made in nessie).

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n akri -l app.kubernetes.io/name=akri
kubectl get events -n akri --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Extensibility] HTTP protocol" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Sorry , we have been putting out some fires ... this is still a priority for us to get in!  We have a goal of having someone walk through all of the steps and get it running this week.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with akri installed or the issue environment reproducible.
